risks.title: Controller-worker role blur
risks.description: Execution systems may compete with Archon for orchestration ownership.
mitigation: Centralize planning in archonx-os
Use task envelopes and capability routing
Disallow ad hoc cross-worker orchestration

risks.title: No canonical repo registry
risks.description: Phase 1 inventory was sparse, leaving no stable machine-readable source of repo truth.
mitigation: Create canonical repo registry in archonx-os
Map repo classes to placement and capability tags

risks.title: Pauli stack fragmentation
risks.description: Pauli memory, frontend, and brand layers are not yet normalized into one canonical stack.
mitigation: Designate pauli-comic-funnel as canonical frontend
Use Notion as structured memory backend
Archive duplicate Pauli repos after mapping

risks.title: Desktop privilege sprawl
risks.description: If desktop and shell access are embedded directly in workers, auditability and safety degrade.
mitigation: Use DesktopCommanderMCP as primary control tower
Add approval policies
Route calls through mcp2cli where possible

risks.title: Token inefficiency across tool layers
risks.description: Bypassing mcp2cli leads to larger prompts and duplicated adapter logic.
mitigation: Make mcp2cli mandatory
Track invocation costs and alias usage

risks.title: Environment secret sprawl
risks.description: The env index shows many auth, API, database, cloud, and notion variables with no canonical ownership map.
mitigation: Manage env by category and owning service
Create per-layer env manifests
Avoid raw secret duplication across repos

risks.title: Goose is blind to repo inventory and extensions
risks.description: Cloud coding workflows cannot be trusted until they ingest the canonical repo and extension manifests.
mitigation: Publish repo inventory API
Publish extension registry API
Whitelist canonical Goose extensions

risks.title: White-label readiness blocked by brand-core coupling
risks.description: If The Pauli Effect stays intertwined with the platform core, the system will not scale into a reusable product architecture.
mitigation: Keep Archon-X generic
Make products consume shared services
Separate brand content and product configs from core
